data.title: Siemens’ Automation and Energy Storage Push Might Change The Case For Investing In Siemens (XTRA:SIE)
data.description:
• In late January 2026, Siemens was highlighted across several industry reports for advancing robotics for data centers, AI-driven construction analytics, innovative electric control panels, and modular battery energy storage systems.
  • These developments underscore how Siemens is embedding automation, AI, and grid-support technologies across infrastructure and energy markets, reinforcing its role as a key technology enabler.
  • We’ll now examine how Siemens’ push into modular battery energy storage and broader automation solutions shapes the company’s investment narrative.

This technology could replace computers: discover 22 stocks that are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

What Is Siemens' Investment Narrative?

For Siemens, the core belief you need to have as a shareholder is that industrial automation, software and electrification will keep tightening their grip on global infrastructure, and that Siemens can keep selling higher‑value systems into that shift. The latest news on robotics for data centers, AI construction analytics, intelligent control panels and modular battery storage fits neatly into that story, but is unlikely to change the near term financial picture on its own. In the short run, the more material angles look like continued execution in software‑rich automation and disciplined capital allocation, including the ongoing share buyback and steadily rising dividend. The flip side is that Siemens already trades on a premium earnings multiple, carries meaningful debt and has seen margins slip, so expectations are not low if growth in these newer areas takes time to show up.

However, investors should also weigh how Siemens’ high valuation and debt could amplify any slowdown. Siemens' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 14%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The Simply Wall St Community’s 11 fair value estimates span about €208 to nearly €299, reflecting very different expectations. Set that against Siemens’ push into AI automation and energy storage, and you can see why many readers may want to stress test their own assumptions before deciding how much of the story they believe.

Explore 11 other fair value estimates on Siemens - why the stock might be worth 19% less than the current price!

data.title: RVRC Holding (OM:RVRC) Margin Drift To 15.5% Tests Growth Focus After Q2 Earnings
data.description:
RVRC Holding (OM:RVRC) has reported a busy Q2 2026, with revenue of SEK726 million and EPS of SEK1.30 setting the tone for its latest results. The company’s revenue increased from SEK684 million in Q2 2025 to SEK726 million in Q2 2026, while EPS over the same quarters moved from SEK1.12 to SEK1.30. Trailing twelve month EPS of SEK2.85 frames the current earnings power. For investors, a key consideration now is how the solid top line and EPS profile compare with a net profit margin that has eased over the past year.

See our full analysis for RVRC Holding.

With the latest numbers in hand, the next step is to consider how this earnings picture aligns with the prevailing growth focused narrative around RVRC, and where the data might challenge those expectations.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Margins Ease To 15.5% On Trailing Basis

  • RVRC earned SEK312 million in net income on SEK2,008 million of trailing twelve month revenue, which works out to a 15.5% net profit margin compared with 16.2% a year earlier.
  • For a generally bullish view that focuses on growth, a key point is that this 15.5% margin is slightly lower than the prior 16.2%. Investors weighing the growth story against profitability can see that earnings power is supported more by SEK2,008 million of trailing revenue than by margin expansion.
    • Supporters of the growth angle can point to trailing EPS of SEK2.85 alongside that 15.5% margin, showing that absolute profits of SEK312 million are still healthy even as margin has eased.
    • On the other hand, anyone worried about margin pressure can see a clear data point in the shift from 16.2% to 15.5%, which they may compare against the revenue trend when judging how repeatable the current earnings profile looks.

Q2 Net Income Of SEK140 Million Stands Out

  • In Q2 2026, RVRC reported SEK140 million of net income on SEK726 million of revenue, versus SEK125 million on SEK684 million in Q2 2025, so this single quarter contributed a sizeable share of the SEK312 million earned over the last twelve months.
  • For a generally bullish growth angle, this Q2 snapshot with SEK140 million of profit sits within a run of quarterly net income figures between SEK46 million and SEK140 million and EPS between SEK0.41 and SEK1.30. Investors who like the growth story can point to multiple profitable quarters while also seeing that performance does move around within the year.
    • The sequence of quarterly revenue figures from SEK350 million to SEK726 million, paired with positive net income in each quarter, supports the idea that RVRC is producing consistent profits rather than relying on a single period.
    • At the same time, the spread between the lower profit quarters, such as SEK46 million, and Q2's SEK140 million encourages investors to pay attention to seasonality and not assume that the strongest quarter reflects a full year run rate.
To see how this earnings pattern fits into the broader growth story, including risks and valuation, you can read the full narrative range for RVRC Holding. 📊 Read the full RVRC Holding Consensus Narrative.

Growth Metrics Contrast With Mixed Valuation Signals

  • Over the past five years, earnings grew about 9.4% per year and are forecast at roughly 11.6% per year, while revenue is forecast at about 9.9% per year. The current P/E of 23x sits between a peer average of 39x and a European Specialty Retail average of 17.3x, and the share price of SEK68.25 is about 19.5% below the cited DCF fair value of SEK84.82.
  • For investors who lean bullish, the combination of multi year earnings growth around 9.4% per year, forecast revenue growth of about 9.9% per year and a share price below the DCF fair value while the P/E remains above the broader industry average presents a mix of supportive growth data and valuation trade offs that they need to weigh carefully.
    • The P/E discount to the 39x peer average can be seen as aligning with the idea that the stock is not priced at the top of its group even after several years of earnings growth.
    • However, the premium to the 17.3x European Specialty Retail average and the recent margin move from 16.2% to 15.5% give investors concrete numbers to consider when deciding how much weight to place on the growth and DCF signals.

Next Steps

Don't just look at this quarter; the real story is in the long-term trend. We've done an in-depth analysis on RVRC Holding's growth and its valuation to see if today's price is a bargain. Add the company to your watchlist or portfolio now so you don't miss the next big move.

See What Else Is Out There

RVRC’s easing net margin from 16.2% to 15.5% and mixed P/E signals against peers highlight that the growth story comes with valuation trade offs.

If that mix of softer margins and an uncertain valuation trade off does not quite fit your comfort zone, use these 868 undervalued stocks based on cash flows today to focus on companies where pricing looks more clearly aligned with their financial profile.

data.title: Is New Found Gold’s (TSXV:NFG) EPCM Move Quietly Redefining Its Risk Profile And Capital Needs?
data.description:
• New Found Gold Corp. has moved its Queensway Gold Project in Newfoundland and Labrador further toward potential production, launching the 2026 drill program, resuming diamond drilling, and signing a Phase 1 engineering, procurement and construction management contract with WSP Canada for mill upgrades and site development.
  • Ongoing infill drilling at the K2 zone, preparation of an updated mineral resource estimate, and progress on environmental permitting together signal a transition from pure exploration toward early-stage project development and financing work.
  • We will now examine how the EPCM contract with WSP Canada and related permitting and drilling activity shape New Found Gold's investment narrative.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 110 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is New Found Gold's Investment Narrative?

For someone considering New Found Gold, the core belief is that Queensway can progress from a promising discovery into a viable producing asset, despite the company’s lack of revenue and ongoing losses. The new EPCM contract with WSP Canada, the planned Pine Cove mill upgrades, and the active 2026 drill program all push the story further into early development, adding clearer short term catalysts around permitting milestones, the updated mineral resource estimate, and future technical reports. At the same time, this accelerates exposure to execution risk: a relatively new management and board must now manage complex engineering, permitting and future financing decisions, in a market that has recently marked the shares down after a strong 1 year run. Whether the recent pullback reflects opportunity or concern will depend on confidence in that transition.

Yet this pivot toward development introduces a risk many investors may not have fully considered.

Despite retreating, New Found Gold's shares might still be trading 28% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly CA$0.49 to CA$4.86 per share, underscoring how differently private investors view Queensway’s potential. Set that against management’s rapid move into EPCM, permitting and resource updates, and you can see why opinions on New Found Gold’s next phase and its impact on future performance are so far apart. Readers may want to weigh several of these perspectives before forming their own view.

data.title: Assessing Innodata (INOD) Valuation After New Palantir AI Data Engineering Engagement

See our latest analysis for Innodata.

The Palantir announcement comes shortly after Innodata was named to the Missile Defense Agency’s SHIELD program. Yet the stock has seen a 13.19% one day share price decline and a 25.69% 90 day share price return drop, even as its one year total shareholder return sits at 49.51% and the five year total shareholder return is more than 7x. This suggests that long term momentum has been strong while shorter term sentiment has cooled.

If this kind of AI focused contract wins has your attention, it could be a good moment to scan other opportunities across high growth tech and AI stocks and see what else fits your watchlist.

At a last close of $55.44 against a most followed fair value of $93.75, the current price sits well below what this narrative suggests.

Increasing adoption of AI across industries requires curated and high quality datasets, and Innodata's evolving role from simple data provider to strategic partner (sitting "at the table" with clients' data scientists) is likely to support premium pricing, recurring contracts, and market share gains, with positive impact on both revenue stability and net margins.

data.title: EQT’s Growing Scale Reliability And Community Focus In Investor Spotlight
data.description:
• EQT recently became the sixth-largest oil and gas producer in the US, with a growing presence among Appalachian producers.
  • The company maintained stable output during severe winter storms that disrupted parts of the US energy sector.
  • Through the EQT Foundation, the company has recently supported K-12 STEM and arts education programs as part of its community focus.

For investors watching NYSE:EQT, these developments come with a share price of $57.73 and a reported multi year total return of 14.3% over the past year and 266.9% over five years. EQT's scale as a top six US producer, combined with its Appalachian focus, places it in a prominent spot among US natural gas companies.

The recent storm performance and philanthropic activity may influence how you think about EQT's risk profile and corporate priorities. As you follow future updates, you might consider how operational reliability and community engagement fit into your broader thesis on the stock and the energy sector overall.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for EQT by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on EQT.

Why EQT could be great value

EQT’s move into the sixth-largest slot among US oil and gas producers, combined with stable output through severe winter storms, signals to investors that the company is operating at true large cap scale with an emphasis on reliability. For a gas-focused producer competing with peers such as Chesapeake Energy and Coterra, the ability to keep volumes steady when infrastructure is under stress can matter for how the market views cash flow resilience and contract reliability.

Advertisement

EQT narrative, scale and social footprint

The grant activity through the EQT Foundation lines up with existing investor narratives that focus on long-term gas demand from power and AI data centers, because it shows the company trying to maintain a social licence to operate in the Appalachian communities where that growth would occur. For readers following the bullish and consensus EQT narratives, this mix of operational scale, energy transition positioning and visible community spending can be seen as part of the broader story around how EQT tries to differentiate itself from other natural gas names.

Risks and rewards that investors are weighing

  • Growing into a top-six US producer reinforces EQT’s relevance to larger buyers and counterparties, which can help when negotiating long-term contracts.
  • Operational resilience during the cold front supports the view that EQT’s integrated Appalachian footprint can keep gas flowing when conditions are difficult.
  • Larger scale and higher profile can draw more scrutiny to environmental practices and capital allocation, which may influence future project approvals and costs.
  • Philanthropic spending, while relatively small, still competes with other uses of cash such as debt reduction or shareholder returns if commodity conditions soften.

What to watch next

From here, you might watch how EQT talks about winter storm performance, community investment and its integrated gas projects on upcoming calls, and whether those points show up as a factor in analyst commentary compared with peers like EOG Resources and Devon Energy. If you want to see how this latest news fits into the broader long-term story investors are debating, take a look at community narratives on EQT and compare the assumptions that different analysts are using.

data.title: Aurubis AG's (ETR:NDA) Stock On An Uptrend: Could Fundamentals Be Driving The Momentum?
data.description:
Most readers would already be aware that Aurubis' (ETR:NDA) stock increased significantly by 42% over the past three months. As most would know, fundamentals are what usually guide market price movements over the long-term, so we decided to look at the company's key financial indicators today to determine if they have any role to play in the recent price movement. Specifically, we decided to study Aurubis' ROE in this article.

Return on equity or ROE is an important factor to be considered by a shareholder because it tells them how effectively their capital is being reinvested. In other words, it is a profitability ratio which measures the rate of return on the capital provided by the company's shareholders.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

How Do You Calculate Return On Equity?

Return on equity can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for Aurubis is:

11% = €539m ÷ €5.0b (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' is the amount earned after tax over the last twelve months. That means that for every €1 worth of shareholders' equity, the company generated €0.11 in profit.

View our latest analysis for Aurubis

Why Is ROE Important For Earnings Growth?

Thus far, we have learned that ROE measures how efficiently a company is generating its profits. Depending on how much of these profits the company reinvests or "retains", and how effectively it does so, we are then able to assess a company’s earnings growth potential. Assuming everything else remains unchanged, the higher the ROE and profit retention, the higher the growth rate of a company compared to companies that don't necessarily bear these characteristics.

Aurubis' Earnings Growth And 11% ROE

To begin with, Aurubis seems to have a respectable ROE. On comparing with the average industry ROE of 8.4% the company's ROE looks pretty remarkable. As you might expect, the 2.7% net income decline reported by Aurubis is a bit of a surprise. We reckon that there could be some other factors at play here that are preventing the company's growth. Such as, the company pays out a huge portion of its earnings as dividends, or is faced with competitive pressures.

However, when we compared Aurubis' growth with the industry we found that while the company's earnings have been shrinking, the industry has seen an earnings growth of 2.3% in the same period. This is quite worrisome.

The basis for attaching value to a company is, to a great extent, tied to its earnings growth. The investor should try to establish if the expected growth or decline in earnings, whichever the case may be, is priced in. This then helps them determine if the stock is placed for a bright or bleak future. What is NDA worth today? The intrinsic value infographic in our free research report helps visualize whether NDA is currently mispriced by the market.

Is Aurubis Using Its Retained Earnings Effectively?

Aurubis' low three-year median payout ratio of 16% (or a retention ratio of 84%) over the last three years should mean that the company is retaining most of its earnings to fuel its growth but the company's earnings have actually shrunk. The low payout should mean that the company is retaining most of its earnings and consequently, should see some growth. So there could be some other explanations in that regard. For example, the company's business may be deteriorating.

Moreover, Aurubis has been paying dividends for at least ten years or more suggesting that management must have perceived that the shareholders prefer dividends over earnings growth. Looking at the current analyst consensus data, we can see that the company's future payout ratio is expected to rise to 26% over the next three years. Accordingly, the expected increase in the payout ratio explains the expected decline in the company's ROE to 7.6%, over the same period.

Summary

On the whole, we do feel that Aurubis has some positive attributes. However, given the high ROE and high profit retention, we would expect the company to be delivering strong earnings growth, but that isn't the case here. This suggests that there might be some external threat to the business, that's hampering its growth. Additionally, the latest industry analyst forecasts show that analysts expect the company's earnings to continue to shrink in the future. Are these analysts expectations based on the broad expectations for the industry, or on the company's fundamentals? Click here to be taken to our analyst's forecasts page for the company.

data.title: Should Coupang’s (CPNG) US$900 Million Data-Breach Penalty Risk Require Action From Investors?
data.description:
• In recent months, Coupang has been hit by multiple securities class action filings and regulatory probes after a data breach exposed tens of millions of customer accounts and allegedly went undisclosed for nearly six months.
  • The fallout now extends beyond cybersecurity, as South Korea’s Fair Trade Commission weighs very large penalties while US officials pressure Seoul over its treatment of American tech firms like Coupang.
  • Against this backdrop of mounting regulatory risk, we’ll examine how the potential US$900 million fine shapes Coupang’s investment narrative.

We've found 14 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Coupang's Investment Narrative?

To own Coupang today, you really have to believe that its core e‑commerce and logistics engine can keep compounding through volatility, while management absorbs a hit from the data breach without derailing the business. Before the scandal, the big catalysts were earnings growth expectations, continued profitability after a long path to the black, and capital returns via buybacks, all while the stock traded well below consensus fair value. The alleged six‑month, undisclosed breach and potential US$900 million KFTC fine now push legal and regulatory outcomes to the front of the story, at least in the short term, crowding out cleaner earnings and growth narratives. The recent share price resilience, despite sharp earnings downgrades, suggests the market is still weighing how permanent this damage will be.

However, one regulatory decision could sharply reshape Coupang’s near term profit profile and risk. Coupang's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 15%. Find out what it's worth.

Eight members of the Simply Wall St Community see Coupang’s fair value between about US$23.85 and US$40, yet the unresolved data breach penalties and class actions could materially influence how those projections play out. data.title: PAR Technology’s Bridg Deal Deepens Data Focus As Shares Screen Undervalued
data.description:
• PAR Technology (NYSE:PAR) has agreed to acquire Bridg, a customer data and shopper intelligence platform.
  • The deal is intended to integrate identity resolution and customer analytics into PAR Technology's existing restaurant and retail solutions.
  • The acquisition focuses on connecting in store and online transaction data to support more targeted marketing and loyalty programs.

PAR Technology operates as a provider of software, hardware, and services for restaurant and retail operators, with a focus on point of sale and loyalty tools. By adding Bridg's shopper intelligence capabilities, the company is looking to deepen its role in how merchants understand and interact with guests across channels. For investors, this adds another layer to PAR's data story within an industry that is increasingly focused on personalization and digital engagement.

Looking ahead, the key questions are how smoothly Bridg's technology is integrated into PAR's platform and how quickly customers adopt the combined offering. You may want to watch for updates on customer wins, product roadmap progress, and how PAR explains the role of data and analytics in its long term strategy.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for PAR Technology by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on PAR Technology.

data.title: Did ADM’s US$40 Million SEC Settlement and Governance Reset Just Shift Archer-Daniels-Midland's (ADM) Investment Narrative?
data.description:
• Archer-Daniels-Midland recently agreed to pay a US$40,000,000 civil penalty to settle a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission investigation into prior intersegment sales reporting in its Nutrition business, while the Department of Justice closed its separate probe with no further action.
  • Although the transactions required restatements of segment-level results and prompted leadership and control changes, ADM reported no impact on consolidated earnings, cash flows, or the balance sheet, highlighting a governance issue more than a balance-sheet one.
  • With the regulatory overhang now resolved and new financial controls in place, we’ll examine how this compliance reset influences ADM’s investment narrative.

These 13 companies survived and thrived after COVID and have the right ingredients to survive Trump's tariffs. Discover why before your portfolio feels the trade war pinch.

Advertisement

What Is Archer-Daniels-Midland's Investment Narrative?

For ADM to make sense in a portfolio today, I think you have to believe in a fairly simple story: a global ag processor with modest growth prospects, pressured margins, but solid cash generation that can still support dividends and buybacks. Recent results show earnings under strain, even as the company continues returning capital to shareholders and trades at a richer multiple than many food peers. Against that backdrop, the SEC settlement looks more like a clean-up of past governance issues than a new financial overhang, especially given the lack of impact on consolidated earnings and cash flows and the relatively small US$40,000,000 penalty versus ADM’s revenue base of more than US$80 billion. In the near term, the key catalysts remain earnings stabilization, capital returns and any shift in how investors price the governance risk premium after this compliance reset.

However, the accounting episode still leaves a question mark around trust in segment disclosures that investors should not ignore. Archer-Daniels-Midland's share price has been on the slide but might be dropping deeper into value territory. Find out whether it's a bargain at this price.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Twelve fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly US$29 to US$73 per share, reflecting very different expectations for ADM’s upside. Set that against the recent governance issues and earnings pressure, and it becomes clear that taking in multiple viewpoints can change how you think about the payoff from holding this stock.

data.title: Premier Financial Integration And Scale Expansion Could Be A Game Changer For WesBanco (WSBC)
data.description:
• WesBanco, Inc. recently reported fourth-quarter 2025 net income of US$91.11 million on net interest income of US$222.3 million, capping a full year in which net income reached US$223.11 million and net interest income rose to US$814.3 million following its Premier Financial acquisition.
  • While full-year basic earnings per share from continuing operations were broadly flat at US$2.23, the Premier Financial integration significantly expanded WesBanco’s franchise to about US$28 billion in assets and supported strong loan and deposit growth alongside improved efficiency.
  • We’ll now examine how WesBanco’s expanded scale from the Premier Financial integration shapes its investment narrative in light of these results.

Uncover the next big thing with financially sound penny stocks that balance risk and reward.

Advertisement

What Is WesBanco's Investment Narrative?

To own WesBanco after the Premier Financial deal, you have to believe that a larger, more efficient regional bank can turn scale into sustainably higher earnings without taking on outsized credit or integration risk. The latest results support that core idea: net interest income and net income stepped up meaningfully with Premier folded in, even though full year EPS was broadly flat and included a very large one off loss. Near term, the key catalyst is whether management can translate this bigger balance sheet and improving efficiency ratio into cleaner, more predictable earnings as integration costs fade and loan growth normalizes. The Q4 numbers help here, while the slight EPS miss and ongoing commercial real estate payoffs keep asset quality and margin trends firmly in focus. The planned retirement of the long serving Chief Risk Officer in 2026 also adds an extra governance angle for investors to watch, even if it is not an immediate financial swing factor.

However, the upcoming Chief Risk Officer transition is something shareholders should keep an eye on. WesBanco's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 49%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Four Simply Wall St Community fair value estimates for WesBanco span from about US$38.57 to nearly US$69.21 per share, reflecting sharply different views on what its expanded US$28 billion franchise is worth. When you set these against the recent earnings uplift from Premier and the ongoing questions around commercial real estate payoffs and risk oversight, it underlines why many market participants are weighing both growth potential and balance sheet quality before taking a strong stance.

data.title: Is Constellation Brands (STZ) Pricing Reflect Its Recent Share Rebound And Premium Beer Focus
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Constellation Brands’ current share price lines up with its underlying worth, you are not alone. This article is built to help you frame that question clearly.
  • The stock last closed at US$156.70, with returns of 13.6% over the past month, 11.0% year to date and a 10.6% decline over the past year. These figures can change how you think about both growth potential and risk.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Constellation Brands’ position in the premium beer and spirits market and how investor sentiment has reacted to that focus. This helps explain some of the shorter term share price moves and provides context when you are trying to decide whether the latest returns reflect changing expectations or simply short term swings in sentiment.
  • On Simply Wall St’s valuation checks, Constellation Brands scores 2 out of 6 for being assessed as undervalued. Next we will look at how different valuation methods arrive at that view and why there may be an even more helpful way to think about value by the end of this article.

Constellation Brands scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Constellation Brands Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes the cash Constellation Brands is expected to generate in the future and discounts those projections back to what they might be worth in today’s dollars.

Simply Wall St uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model here. Constellation Brands last reported free cash flow of about $1.59b. Based on analyst inputs for the earlier years and extrapolations after that, free cash flow is projected at $2.35b by 2030, with a series of annual projections between 2026 and 2035 that are discounted back to today using the model’s required return assumptions.

When all of those discounted cash flows are added up, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of US$312.56 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$156.70, this implies the stock is assessed as 49.9% undervalued using this DCF framework.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Constellation Brands is undervalued by 49.9%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 868 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Constellation Brands.

Approach 2: Constellation Brands Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Constellation Brands, the P/E ratio is a useful way to link what you pay for the stock to the earnings it currently generates. It helps you see how many dollars investors are willing to pay today for each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a "normal" P/E really depends on how investors view the company’s growth potential and risk. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while more uncertainty or lower growth expectations usually point to a lower P/E.

Constellation Brands currently trades on a P/E of 24.49x. That sits above the Beverage industry average of 17.69x and above the peer group average of 19.16x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for the stock is 21.64x, which is a proprietary estimate of what the P/E might be given factors like earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and company specific risks.

The Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers because it adjusts for those company specific drivers rather than assuming all Beverage stocks should trade on the same multiple. With the actual P/E of 24.49x sitting above the Fair Ratio of 21.64x, the shares screen as overvalued on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1417 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Constellation Brands Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, which are simply your story about Constellation Brands linked directly to your numbers on fair value, expected revenue, earnings and margins.

A Narrative connects three pieces in one place: how you think the business will develop, what that implies for future financials, and the fair value those cash flows could support.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives sit in the Community page and are designed so any investor can quickly set assumptions, see the fair value those assumptions produce, and compare it with the current share price to help decide whether to buy, hold or sell.

Narratives also update when new information like news or earnings is added. If several Constellation Brands Narratives show very different fair values, that reflects investors using the same tool to express different expectations about its premium beer and spirits position and how that could translate into future results.

Do you think there's more to the story for Constellation Brands? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: €1,353 - That's What Analysts Think ASML Holding N.V. (AMS:ASML) Is Worth After These Results
data.description:
Investors in ASML Holding N.V. (AMS:ASML) had a good week, as its shares rose 3.2% to close at €1,216 following the release of its annual results. ASML Holding reported in line with analyst predictions, delivering revenues of €33b and statutory earnings per share of €24.71, suggesting the business is executing well and in line with its plan. The analysts typically update their forecasts at each earnings report, and we can judge from their estimates whether their view of the company has changed or if there are any new concerns to be aware of. So we collected the latest post-earnings statutory consensus estimates to see what could be in store for next year.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Taking into account the latest results, the current consensus from ASML Holding's 38 analysts is for revenues of €37.0b in 2026. This would reflect a notable 13% increase on its revenue over the past 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to ascend 17% to €29.21. Before this earnings report, the analysts had been forecasting revenues of €35.3b and earnings per share (EPS) of €27.70 in 2026. It looks like there's been a modest increase in sentiment following the latest results, withthe analysts becoming a bit more optimistic in their predictions for both revenues and earnings.

See our latest analysis for ASML Holding

It will come as no surprise to learn that the analysts have increased their price target for ASML Holding 12% to €1,353on the back of these upgrades. There's another way to think about price targets though, and that's to look at the range of price targets put forward by analysts, because a wide range of estimates could suggest a diverse view on possible outcomes for the business. There are some variant perceptions on ASML Holding, with the most bullish analyst valuing it at €1,600 and the most bearish at €940 per share. As you can see, analysts are not all in agreement on the stock's future, but the range of estimates is still reasonably narrow, which could suggest that the outcome is not totally unpredictable.

These estimates are interesting, but it can be useful to paint some more broad strokes when seeing how forecasts compare, both to the ASML Holding's past performance and to peers in the same industry. The period to the end of 2026 brings more of the same, according to the analysts, with revenue forecast to display 13% growth on an annualised basis. That is in line with its 15% annual growth over the past five years. Compare this with the broader industry, which analyst estimates (in aggregate) suggest will see revenues grow 11% annually. So although ASML Holding is expected to maintain its revenue growth rate, it's only growing at about the rate of the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing here is that the analysts upgraded their earnings per share estimates, suggesting that there has been a clear increase in optimism towards ASML Holding following these results. They also upgraded their revenue forecasts, although the latest estimates suggest that ASML Holding will grow in line with the overall industry. There was also a nice increase in the price target, with the analysts clearly feeling that the intrinsic value of the business is improving.

With that said, the long-term trajectory of the company's earnings is a lot more important than next year. We have estimates - from multiple ASML Holding analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

And what about risks? Every company has them, and we've spotted 2 warning signs for ASML Holding you should know about.

data.title: KLA (KLAC) Net Margin At 35.8% Tests Justification For Premium Earnings Narrative
data.description:
KLA (KLAC) has just posted Q2 2026 results with revenue of US$3.3 billion and basic EPS of US$8.73, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of US$12.7 billion and EPS of US$34.55 that sit against a 42.1% earnings rise over the past year. Over recent quarters the company has seen revenue move from US$3.1 billion in Q2 2025 to US$3.3 billion in Q2 2026, while quarterly basic EPS shifted from US$6.18 to US$8.73. This sets up the latest report against a backdrop of faster trailing earnings growth than its five year average. With net profit margins also higher than a year ago, the story this quarter is about how much of that profitability investors think can stick.

See our full analysis for KLA.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how they line up with the widely followed narratives around KLA’s growth, quality of earnings, and long term profit profile.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

35.8% net margin keeps profits thick

  • Over the last 12 months, KLA turned US$12.7b of revenue into US$4.6b of net income, which works out to a 35.8% net profit margin versus 29.6% a year earlier.
  • What really backs the bullish view on earnings quality is that trailing EPS of US$34.55 is tied to this higher margin profile, while quarterly net income stayed above US$1.1b across Q1 and Q2 2026. This suggests the recent 42.1% year on year earnings growth is not just a one quarter spike.
    • Bulls often argue that high margins give KLA more room to handle swings in chip equipment spending, and the move from 29.6% to 35.8% margins is consistent with that view.
    • At the same time, anyone cautious can see that profitability is already at a high level, so future gains would need to build on an already strong base rather than a weak starting point.
📊 Read the full KLA Consensus Narrative.

EPS trend backs 42.1% earnings growth

  • Across the last six reported quarters, basic EPS moved from US$6.18 in Q2 2025 to US$8.73 in Q2 2026, with trailing twelve month EPS rising from US$22.01 in Q1 2025 to US$34.55 in Q2 2026 alongside a 42.1% earnings increase over the past year versus a 5 year average of 11.7% per year.
  • Supporters of a more bullish angle on KLA’s business argue that this pattern shows the company compounding off a higher base, and the data gives them some backing because EPS stayed above US$8.20 in four of the last five quarters while trailing revenue climbed from US$10.3b to US$12.7b over the same period.
    • That said, the earnings growth rate cited going forward, at around 14.4% per year, sits below the 42.1% achieved in the last 12 months, which highlights that recent growth has been stronger than the medium term average of 11.7% a year.
    • So while the numbers heavily support a bullish case built on strong execution over the last year, they also set a high bar if future growth settles closer to the lower forecast pace.

P/E of 41.2x versus DCF fair value

  • KLA is trading at US$1,427.94 per share, which implies a trailing P/E of 41.2x that sits below the peer average of 48.4x and close to the semiconductor industry at 41.9x, while the supplied DCF fair value is US$739.11, well under the current share price.
  • Critics who lean bearish point to the gap between price and DCF fair value, and the numbers give that concern some support because the share price is almost twice the DCF estimate. This is the case even though forecasts in the data call for earnings growth of about 14.4% per year and revenue growth of about 10.5% per year, both slightly below the referenced US market benchmarks.
    • At the same time, the P/E being lower than the 48.4x peer average shows that on this one metric KLA does not look stretched against similar companies, which complicates a simple bearish read on valuation.
    • The tension for investors is that stronger trailing margins and a 42.1% earnings jump are already reflected in the price at 41.2x earnings, while the DCF fair value figure anchors a more cautious stance on how much of that strength is worth paying for.

Next Steps

Don't just look at this quarter; the real story is in the long-term trend. We've done an in-depth analysis on KLA's growth and its valuation to see if today's price is a bargain. Add the company to your watchlist or portfolio now so you don't miss the next big move.

See What Else Is Out There

KLA’s strong recent earnings sit against a P/E of 41.2x and a share price that is almost twice the supplied DCF fair value.

What Royal Caribbean’s Recent Performance Tells You

Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL) has drawn attention after recent share price swings, including a 6% decline over the past day, while gains over the past week, month, and past 3 months remain in positive territory.

At a last close of US$324.65, the cruise operator behind the Royal Caribbean International, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea brands is being assessed by investors in relation to its recent revenue of US$17.9b and net income of US$4.3b.

See our latest analysis for Royal Caribbean Cruises.

That 6.2% one day share price decline comes after a run of strong momentum, including a 16.4% 30 day share price return and a 23.3% one year total shareholder return, which points to interest building rather than fading.

If Royal Caribbean’s moves have caught your eye, this can be a good moment to broaden your search and check out auto manufacturers as another corner of the market to research.

With RCL trading at US$324.65 against recent revenue of US$17.9b, net income of US$4.3b and an analyst price target of US$346.33, should you see value here or assume the market is already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 1.9% Undervalued

Royal Caribbean’s most followed narrative places fair value at about $331.04, slightly above the last close of $324.65. This frames the current debate around its longer term upside.

The introduction of new ships like Star of the Seas and Celebrity Xcel, coupled with existing fleet performance, is expected to drive yield growth between 2.6% and 4.6% in 2025, positively impacting revenue and earnings. Enhanced guest experiences, investments in private destinations, and new ships are driving higher onboard spending and pre-cruise purchases, which should support revenue growth by increasing per-passenger spend.

Read the complete narrative.

The relatively small gap between price and fair value is based on several elements. Revenue, earnings and margins are all doing significant work in this story. The discount rate, future profit margins and capacity assumptions all contribute to the $331.04 figure. This framework shows how those moving parts are expected to fit together over the next few years.

However, this depends on consumer spending and pricing power holding up, as weaker demand or tougher price competition could quickly challenge those fair value assumptions.

data.title: Lloyds Banking Group (LSE:LLOY) Valuation Check After Strong Recent Share Price Returns
data.description:
Advertisement

Why Lloyds Banking Group is on investor watchlists today

Lloyds Banking Group (LSE:LLOY) has drawn fresh interest after recent share price gains over the month and past 3 months, prompting investors to reassess how its current valuation lines up with fundamentals.

See our latest analysis for Lloyds Banking Group.

Recent trading has been strong, with a 1 day share price return of 3.32% and a 90 day share price return of 22.31%. The 1 year total shareholder return of 83.53% and 5 year total shareholder return of 271.01% point to momentum that has been building rather than fading.

If Lloyds' move has you rethinking where the next big gains might come from, this could be a good moment to widen your search with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

So with Lloyds trading close to some analyst targets yet still showing a sizeable intrinsic discount, should you view today’s price as leaving meaningful upside on the table, or has the market already priced in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 14% Overvalued

The most followed valuation narrative puts Lloyds' fair value at £0.96 per share, compared with the last close of £1.09, and builds its case on earnings, margins and capital returns.

Operational leverage from cost discipline, ongoing investment in AI and data analytics, and successful execution of cross-division growth initiatives, particularly in bancassurance, insurance, and fee-generating "capital-lite" businesses, are expected to support strong capital generation, improved net margins, and resilient ROE as Lloyds transitions more of its earnings base away from low-growth, commoditized lending activities.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what kind of earnings path, margin profile and future profit multiple are needed to back that £0.96 figure? The core narrative leans heavily on steady revenue growth, fatter margins and shrinking share count. Want to see exactly how those moving parts combine into today’s fair value call?

Result: Fair Value of £0.96 (OVERVALUED)

However, this hinges on the UK economy holding up and Lloyds keeping pace with fintech competitors, as weaker growth or faster digital disruption could quickly challenge that story.

data.title: Evaluating Flex (FLEX) After Raymond James Upgrade On Cloud And AI Growth Potential
data.description:
A Raymond James upgrade has put Flex (FLEX) back on investors’ radar after the firm cited its role in cloud and AI infrastructure and highlighted differentiated power solutions for hyperscale data centers and higher margin power operations.

See our latest analysis for Flex.

Flex’s recent pullback, with a 1 day share price return of 3.71% decline to US$63.04, comes after a steadier 30 day share price return of 4.34% and a much stronger 1 year total shareholder return of 51.36%. This suggests longer term momentum remains intact despite short term volatility around the Raymond James upgrade and the focus on its cloud and AI infrastructure exposure.

If Flex’s AI and data center story has your attention, it could be a good moment to scan other high growth tech and AI stocks that are also tied to these themes.

Flex’s shares now sit at a modest 4% intrinsic discount and about a 17% gap to analyst targets. The stock has already delivered a 51.36% 1-year return, so is there still a buying opportunity here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 14.5% Undervalued

Flex's most followed narrative points to a fair value of about $73.70 versus the last close at $63.04, putting the current discount clearly in focus.

The ongoing surge in demand for data center and AI infrastructure requiring integrated power, cooling, and advanced IT hardware positions Flex for sustained, outsized revenue growth, as evidenced by the 35% forecasted annual increase in its data center segment, supporting both topline expansion and higher portfolio margins. Flex's global manufacturing footprint, especially its ability to regionalize and rapidly scale production across North America and Europe, aligns with accelerating customer efforts to de risk and diversify supply chains.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what sits behind that fair value gap? The narrative leans heavily on earnings expansion, margin uplift, and a re rated earnings multiple tied to AI centric revenue.

Result: Fair Value of $73.70 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, that upside story still hinges on concentrated hyperscaler clients and thin margins, so any insourcing or pricing pressure could quickly challenge those fair value assumptions.
